segment_policy_doc.name: Item 1 — Segment reporting overview
segment_policy_doc.excerpt:
Item 1. Business

We have organized our operations into three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services ("AWS"). These segments reflect the way the Company evaluates its business performance and manages its operations.

Information on our net sales is contained in Item 8 of Part II, "Financial Statements and Supplementary Data — Note 10 — Segment Information."

Certain operating expense line items, including stock-based compensation, are not allocated to segment results when measuring segment operating performance.

compensation_disclosure_doc.name: Note 8 — Stockholders' Equity (SBC expense)
compensation_disclosure_doc.excerpt:
Note 8 — Stockholders' Equity

Stock Award Plans

Stock-based compensation expense is as follows (in millions):

Year Ended December 31, 2025

Total stock-based compensation expense $ 19,467

We measure segment operating performance before stock-based compensation expense; these amounts are not allocated to North America, International, or AWS segment results.

narrative_fx_doc.name: MD&A — International net sales and FX
narrative_fx_doc.excerpt:
Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

International Segment

International segment sales increased 13% year-over-year to $161.9 billion in 2025, or increased 10% excluding changes in foreign exchange rates.

North America segment sales increased 10% year-over-year to $426.3 billion.

AWS segment sales increased 20% year-over-year to $128.7 billion.
